findings.title: Skill body word count outside target range (target: 1,500-2,000)
findings.snippet:
# Ralph-Loop × Analyze-Codebase

Iteratively scan codebase dimensions...
findings.description: The ralph-loop SKILL.md body contains approximately 1,247 words, which is below the 1,500-word target minimum. Despite having a references/ directory with 3 files (multi-dimension.md, recovery-protocol.md, verification-scan.md) that offload detail via progressive disclosure, the core body is thin relative to the skill's complexity. Key concepts like the gap-weighted priority algorithm and multi-dimension state management are only briefly mentioned before deferring to references.
findings.recommendation: Expand the core workflow steps (Steps 4-9) with more inline detail on the iteration logic, batch selection heuristics, and completion detection. The references/ files handle advanced topics well, but the body should be self-sufficient for single-dimension mode without requiring reference loads.

findings.title: Skill body word count outside target range (target: 1,500-2,000)
findings.snippet:
# Refactor Plan -- Generate Refactoring Plans from Analysis

Generate refactoring plans from existing analysis findings...
findings.description: The refactor-plan SKILL.md body contains approximately 1,228 words, below the 1,500-word target minimum. While the skill delegates most work to the refactoring-planner agent, the body could better document the flag-to-behavior mapping inline rather than deferring entirely to references/flag-behavior.md. The critic feedback loop (Phase 2) is well-documented but the edge cases and error handling sections are thin.
findings.recommendation: Expand the critic feedback loop documentation and inline a summary of how each flag affects agent behavior. The references/flag-behavior.md file can retain the full detail, but a condensed version in the body would improve self-sufficiency.

findings.title: Skill body word count outside target range (target: 1,500-2,000)
findings.snippet:
# Scan Security

## Purpose

Analyze the codebase for security vulnerabilities...
findings.description: The scan-security SKILL.md body contains approximately 1,355 words, below the 1,500-word target minimum. The skill covers 11 workflow steps including OWASP top 10 patterns, plugin-mode security, and CVE checks, but several steps are compressed to 3-4 lines. Steps 6 (Path Traversal), 7 (SSRF), and 8 (Logging) would benefit from expanded grep pattern examples.
findings.recommendation: Expand Steps 6-8 with additional grep pattern examples for each language in the stack. The plugin security steps (P1-P4) are also quite terse and could include example grep commands. Target adding approximately 150-200 words to reach the 1,500-word floor.

findings.title: Skill body word count outside target range (target: 1,500-2,000)
findings.snippet:
# Scan Quality

## Purpose

Evaluate code quality across three sub-areas...
findings.description: The scan-quality SKILL.md body contains approximately 2,273 words, above the 2,000-word target maximum. The skill covers 16 steps spanning code health, tech debt, and performance analysis. While comprehensive, several sections (Steps 11-15 on performance) contain detailed grep patterns that could be extracted to a references/ file.
findings.recommendation: Create a references/ directory and extract the performance-specific grep patterns (Steps 11-15) into references/performance-patterns.md. This would bring the body closer to the 2,000-word target while maintaining progressive disclosure.

findings.title: Skill body exceeds 2,000 words with no references/ directory - extract detailed content
findings.snippet:
# Scan Quality

## Purpose

Evaluate code quality across three sub-areas...
findings.description: The scan-quality skill has 2,273 body words but no references/ directory. The skill contains detailed language-specific grep patterns for 16 steps (code health, tech debt, performance) that load into context on every invocation. Performance patterns (Steps 11-15) and deprecated API patterns (Step 8) are reference material that Claude does not need on every activation.
findings.recommendation: Create skills/scan-quality/references/ with at least two files: performance-patterns.md (Steps 11-15 grep patterns) and deprecated-apis.md (Step 8 patterns). Update the SKILL.md body to reference them conditionally: 'Read references/performance-patterns.md when analyzing data access, pagination, memory, rendering, or caching patterns.'

findings.title: Skill body word count outside target range (target: 1,500-2,000)
findings.snippet:
# Scan Structure

## Purpose

Analyze the codebase's module structure...
findings.description: The scan-structure SKILL.md body contains approximately 1,465 words, marginally below the 1,500-word target minimum (35 words short). The skill covers 10 workflow steps across module structure, design patterns, and error handling. The shortfall is minor and does not indicate insufficient coverage — most steps have adequate detail. Downgraded to info per profile ground truth because the gap is less than 5% of the target minimum.
findings.recommendation: Consider expanding Step 5 (Assess Cohesion) or Step 8 (Check Pattern Consistency) with additional inline examples. A small expansion of 35-50 words would bring the body within target range.

findings.title: Large inline content block should be extracted to a supporting file in references/ or examples/
findings.snippet:
```text
# Standard plugin with skills and agents
my-plugin/
...
```
findings.description: The scan-manifest-structure SKILL.md contains a 31-line fenced text block showing acceptable and unacceptable directory structure examples. While just above the 30-line threshold and serving as procedural reference material (not pure reference documentation), it could be a candidate for extraction if the body grows further.
findings.recommendation: Monitor body length. If future edits push the word count above 2,000, extract the directory structure examples to references/structure-examples.md. Current word count (1,719) is within target range, so no immediate action needed.
